evals.name: create-component-scss
evals.prompt: I'm creating a new ProfileView component at src/views/Profile/ProfileView.vue. It needs a card layout with padding, a header row, and some content. Set up the SCSS file for me.

assertions.text: Creates src/views/Profile/ProfileView.scss (not inline styles)
Uses <style lang="scss" scoped src="./ProfileView.scss"></style> reference
Uses $spacing-* tokens for padding and gaps
Uses mixins like @include flex-col or @include flex-between
Does not include code comments in the SCSS file
Does not add @use or @import statements (auto-injected)

evals.name: responsive-mixin-usage
evals.prompt: I have a grid that should show 1 column on mobile, 2 columns on tablets, and 3 columns on desktop. How do I make it responsive?

assertions.text: Uses @include respond-to('md') for tablet breakpoint
Uses @include respond-to('lg') or respond-to('xl') for desktop
Uses mobile-first approach (base styles are single column)
References correct breakpoint values (md: 768px, lg: 1024px)
Does not use raw @media queries with hardcoded pixel values

evals.name: choose-spacing-token
evals.prompt: What spacing value should I use for the padding inside a card component and the gap between cards in a list?

assertions.text: Recommends $spacing-md (16px) for card internal padding
Recommends $spacing-sm (8px) or $spacing-md (16px) for gap between items
References the spacing scale from variables.scss
Does not suggest hardcoded pixel values
May suggest $spacing-lg for larger sections

evals.name: add-entrance-animation
evals.prompt: I want the feature cards on my landing page to fade in one by one when the page loads. How should I do this?

assertions.text: Recommends .animate-fade-up or .animate-reveal class
Uses .delay-1, .delay-2, .delay-3 classes for staggering
Mentions that prefers-reduced-motion is handled automatically
Shows the class applied in the template HTML
Does not suggest writing custom @keyframes (use existing ones)

evals.name: utility-class-selection
evals.prompt: I need a frosted glass panel with a status badge that says 'Active' and a heading with gradient text. What classes should I use?

assertions.text: Uses .glass or .glass-dark for the frosted panel
Uses .badge .badge-success for the Active status badge
Uses .text-gradient-primary (or secondary/accent) for the heading
References _utilities.scss as the source
Shows correct HTML class application

evals.name: shadow-and-hover-choice
evals.prompt: I have a card that should have subtle elevation and lift slightly when hovered. What's the best approach?

assertions.text: Uses $shadow-md or $shadow-sm for the base shadow
Recommends @include hover-lift mixin OR .hover-lift utility class
Does not use hardcoded box-shadow values
References the transition being included in hover-lift
May mention $shadow-lg as the hover shadow level

evals.name: skeleton-loading-pattern
evals.prompt: I need to show a loading placeholder while my API data loads. The placeholder should look like a card with a title and two lines of text.

assertions.text: Uses the .skeleton utility class
Shows multiple skeleton divs with varying widths for realistic appearance
Mentions the shimmer animation is automatic
Uses appropriate height/width styling for the placeholders
Does not suggest installing a third-party skeleton library
